source.title: Dynamics of Awake Hippocampal-Prefrontal Replay for Spatial Learning and Memory-Guided Decision Making

confidence_rationale: Simultaneous hippocampal-prefrontal ensemble recording in rats during spatial alternation learning. Direct evidence that hippocampal forward replay (during immobility pauses) co-occurs with prefrontal readout of correct future paths and is coordinated across both regions. The reverse/forward asymmetry with learning stage is strong mechanistic evidence for the cue-retrieval model. High source quality (Neuron, in vivo electrophysiology); high mapping fidelity.
failure_signatures: The study uses a constrained linear track / Y-maze, so the generalisation to open-field novel-combination trajectory planning (as in Pfeiffer & Foster 2013) is uncertain.
Causal role of prefrontal readout in guiding decisions is correlational at this stage -- prefrontal sequence activity tracks correct paths but causal necessity is not proven.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Awake hippocampal replay at pause points supports both retrospective evaluation (reverse replay) and prospective planning (forward replay), with coordinated prefrontal activity distinguishing correct future paths from alternatives; learning shifts from reverse- to forward-replay dominance.
mapping.ree_translation: This is direct evidence for the ARC-007 claim that hippocampus provides prospective trajectory templates that are selected by a prefrontal context signal. The coordinated hippocampal-prefrontal activity during pauses is exactly the retrieval mechanism: PFC query context gates which hippocampal sequence template is replayed and broadcast.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The spatial working memory task constrains the trajectory space; REE's open-field planning problem requires broader generalisation. The 'prefrontal cue -> hippocampal pattern' directionality is not directly shown -- the PFC may be reading rather than querying.
mapping.source_context: In vivo electrophysiology in rats; simultaneous 200+ neuron recordings in CA1 and mPFC during spatial alternation task; longitudinal across learning.

confidence_components.notes: Strong mechanistic evidence; slight caveat on causal direction of the hippocampal-prefrontal interaction.
